description: Create the Phase 3 visual token map shell for the native geometry migration.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md with headings for Compact Shell Tokens, Activity Shell Tokens, Expanded Shell Tokens, Hover and Shadow Rules, Path Sources, Seam Rules, and Evidence.
Add a one-sentence scope note that this document only covers visual geometry migration and excludes auth, todo data, review data, and music provider integration.
Check that the markdown headings are unique and the file opens correctly from repository root.

description: Record the Windows compact and activity shell token values needed for the native preview shells.
steps: Trace the compact width branches, `height = 36`, `ACTIVITY_COLLAPSED_WIDTH = 240`, `COLLAPSED_RADIUS_DEFAULT = 22`, `COLLAPSED_RADIUS_ACTIVITY = 50`, `SQUIRCLE_SMOOTHNESS = 3.5`, and `SQUIRCLE_SMOOTHNESS_ACTIVITY = 2.3` in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md that map each compact or activity token to its source constant or branch and expected Phase 3 preview usage.
Check that every new row includes a direct code reference and no placeholder text remains in those two sections.

description: Record the Windows expanded, hover, and shadow token values needed for the native geometry engine.
steps: Trace `expandedWidth = 460`, `expandedMusicHeight = 210`, `expandedAppHeight = 320`, expanded radius `48`, hover scale `1.06`, and the hover or expanded shadow rule in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md for expanded shells, hover behavior, shadow timing, and shadow visibility gates.
Check that the document clearly marks which tokens are preview-only in Phase 3 and which ones are deferred to later business-state work.

description: Record the Windows path-function sources and seam constraints that the native path factory must preserve.
steps: Trace `generateSquirclePath`, `generateOpenSquirclePath`, `generateLeftCapPath`, `generateRightCapPath`, and `generateEarPath` in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Add rows in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md that map each path function to its inputs, output role, and seam-sensitive notes such as the 1px ear overlap behavior.
Check that the document explicitly states Capsule replacement is not acceptable for these shapes and that each row links to a concrete source location.

description: Create the Phase 3 geometry acceptance document shell for preview, path, and pixel verification.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md with headings for Preview Matrix, Path Parity, Pixel Edge Checks, External Display Scaling, Non-Goals, and Evidence.
Add an empty checklist table under each heading with columns for Condition, Expected Result, Evidence, and Status.
Check that the document contains only shell content and no unverified implementation claims.

description: Fill the Phase 3 acceptance document with the five preview shells and explicit non-goal boundaries.
steps: Add Preview Matrix rows in docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md for compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music shell, and expanded app shell.
Add Non-Goals rows that explicitly exclude auth sync, review data, todo data, real music provider integration, and Phase 5 state-machine work from this phase.
Check that each acceptance row names an observable preview result and links back to docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md or 灵动岛迁移方案.md.

description: Add Phase 3 geometry checklist rows to the migration checklist so later phases can gate on visual evidence.
steps: Append a Phase 3 section to docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md for path parity, preview shell coverage, compact-state black-edge checks, ear seam checks, and external-display scaling.
Link each new checklist row to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.
Check that each checklist row has a clear pass condition and that status fields are prepared but not marked complete.

description: Create the native visual-geometry module scaffolding under mac-island for Phase 3 work.
steps: Create a `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/` folder with placeholder files for `IslandVisualTokens.swift`, `IslandVisualState.swift`, `IslandShapeMetrics.swift`, `IslandPathFactory.swift`, `IslandShapeEngine.swift`, and `IslandVisualStatePreview.swift`.
Add the new files to the Xcode project structure without deleting or rewriting any existing Phase 2 window-layer files.
Check that the project references compile with empty type shells and no unresolved file-path references.

description: Add a single Swift token source for compact, activity, expanded, hover, and shadow geometry values.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualTokens.swift` with grouped token definitions for compact, activity, expanded music, expanded app, hover, and shadow behaviors.
Map each Swift token to the corresponding Windows source constant or documented branch from docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.
Check that token names are stable, compile cleanly, and do not depend on auth, reminder, todo, or music-provider state.

description: Add preview-only visual state cases so Phase 3 can render shell variants without business data.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualState.swift` with preview-only cases for compactCollapsed, hoverCollapsed, activityCollapsed, expandedMusic, and expandedApp.
Expose small computed helpers that answer whether a state is expanded, whether shadow is allowed, and which token set should drive the geometry.
Check that the file has no API, auth, reminder scheduler, or music provider dependency.

description: Add shape metrics that turn a preview state plus scale into concrete geometry inputs.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandShapeMetrics.swift` so it resolves width, height, radius, smoothness, ear tension, ear blend height, scale, stroke visibility, and shadow visibility from `IslandVisualState` and a scalar input.
Keep the metrics API independent from business-state enums by accepting only preview-state and scale inputs.
Check that the resolved metrics for the five preview states match the values recorded in docs/mac-island-visual-token-map.md.

description: Port the Windows squircle body path into the native path factory for compact and expanded shells.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateSquirclePath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift` using `Path` or `CGPath` output.
Keep the same width, height, radius, and smoothness inputs used by the Windows baseline so compact and expanded body paths can be generated from shared metrics.
Check the generated body path with at least one compact and one expanded preview input and confirm the top edge stays flat while the lower corners remain continuous.

description: Port the Windows open-stroke path into the native path factory for the states that allow an inner stroke.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateOpenSquirclePath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Keep the path inputs aligned with `IslandShapeMetrics` so the stroke path follows the same shell geometry as the filled body path.
Check that the open-stroke path does not introduce a top-edge line and can be disabled entirely for compact states that must remain fully black.

description: Port the Windows left-cap path so the native shell can keep the compact and expanded left edge profile.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateLeftCapPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Drive the left-cap path from `IslandShapeMetrics` height, radius, and smoothness values instead of hard-coded preview-specific numbers.
Check that the rendered left cap touches the body path without clipping or leaving a transparent seam in both compact and expanded preview shells.

description: Port the Windows right-cap path so the native shell can keep the compact and expanded right edge profile.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateRightCapPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift`.
Drive the right-cap path from the same metrics model used by the left cap so both sides stay symmetric unless the source path says otherwise.
Check that the rendered right cap stays seam-free against the body path in both compact and expanded preview shells.

description: Port the Windows ear path so the native shell keeps the liquid connection and overlap behavior.
steps: Implement the Swift equivalent of `generateEarPath` inside `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandPathFactory.swift` for left and right ear generation.
Preserve the 1px overlap semantics documented in the Windows source so the ears do not visually detach from the body.
Check that both mirrored ears can render from idle, activity, and expanded metrics without exposing a transparent gap at the join.

description: Compose a single shape engine that returns every Phase 3 shell path from shared metrics.
steps: Implement `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandShapeEngine.swift` so one call can resolve body, optional stroke, left cap, right cap, and ear paths for a given `IslandShapeMetrics` input.
Keep the engine isolated from window placement and business-state logic by accepting only geometry inputs and returning path outputs.
Check that `IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` and `IslandRootView.swift` can consume the engine without duplicating any path math.

description: Render the compact collapsed preview shell with native body, cap, and ear geometry.
steps: Implement the compact-collapsed branch in `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` using `IslandShapeEngine` output.
Render the shell as pure black geometry with no debug text, no placeholder text, and no unsupported stroke.
Check the native preview window and confirm the compact collapsed shell is visible and uses the documented compact metrics.

description: Render the hover compact preview shell with the Windows hover scale and shadow behavior.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the hover-collapsed branch uses the same compact geometry plus the `1.06` hover scale.
Apply the Windows hover shadow rule and keep the body fill black while the hover shell is active.
Check the native preview window and confirm hover mode differs only by allowed hover geometry effects rather than by business content.

description: Render the activity compact preview shell with the Windows activity width, radius, and ear profile.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the activity-collapsed branch uses `ACTIVITY_COLLAPSED_WIDTH`, activity smoothness, activity radius, and activity ear metrics.
Keep the preview shell geometry-only with no review cards, todo rows, reminder content, or real activity data.
Check the native preview window and confirm the activity compact shell is visually distinct from the normal compact shell by geometry alone.

description: Render the expanded music preview shell with the Windows expanded music geometry.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the expanded-music branch uses width `460`, height `210`, expanded radius, and expanded ear metrics.
Allow only the stroke and shadow behaviors that are documented for expanded shells in the Windows baseline.
Check the native preview window and confirm the expanded music shell can render without any music provider dependency.

description: Render the expanded app preview shell with the Windows expanded app geometry.
steps: Extend `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/Visual/IslandVisualStatePreview.swift` so the expanded-app branch uses width `460`, height `320`, expanded radius, and expanded ear metrics.
Keep the expanded app preview geometry-only and avoid pulling in review or todo content components during Phase 3.
Check the native preview window and confirm the expanded app shell can render independently from the expanded music shell.

description: Replace the black probe root view with a preview host that can switch between all five Phase 3 shell variants.
steps: Update `mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/UI/IslandRootView.swift` so it hosts `IslandVisualStatePreview` instead of a static black rectangle.
Add a local preview-state switch mechanism that can move between compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music, and expanded app without touching business data layers.
Check the native app window and confirm each preview state can be selected repeatedly without breaking the Phase 2 top attachment.

description: Apply Phase 2 top-band scaling so the visual geometry engine sizes shells correctly on notch and external displays.
steps: Add a Phase 3 scale input based on the Phase 2 top-band metrics, using the migration-plan guidance for `visualScale` rather than hard-coding a single desktop size.
Feed that scale into `IslandShapeMetrics` so compact, activity, and expanded preview shells scale from one geometry pipeline.
Check that the preview shell still attaches to the active top band and that external-display scaling does not exceed the available top width.

description: Update the native panel sizing path so preview shells request their visible size from geometry metrics instead of placeholder presets.
steps: Extend `IslandPanel.swift` and `IslandWindowController.swift` so the visible shell size can come from resolved Phase 3 geometry metrics rather than only from the old placeholder presets.
Keep the top attachment, display recovery, and click-through logic inside the existing Phase 2 window layer while the size source changes.
Check that switching between the five preview shells updates panel size without pushing the window away from the top anchor.

description: Restrict stroke and shadow rendering to the Windows-defined states so compact shells remain fully black.
steps: Centralize the stroke-visibility and shadow-visibility gates in the Phase 3 visual layer instead of scattering them across multiple preview branches.
Ensure compact collapsed and activity compact states render with no unsupported border, no white edge, and no debug decoration, while hover and expanded states follow the documented shadow rules.
Check the native preview window and confirm compact shells remain fully black and expanded or hover shells are the only ones that show the allowed effects.

description: Compare sampled native geometry output against the Windows path baseline and record any intentional differences.
steps: Generate sampled path output for the native body, open-stroke, left-cap, right-cap, and ear paths using the same width, height, radius, smoothness, tension, and blend-height inputs as the Windows functions.
Compare the native samples against a JS baseline generated from front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx for compact, activity, and expanded inputs.
Record the parity result and any intentional deltas in docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md with evidence links.

description: Capture the five Phase 3 preview shells and verify the edge-pixel requirements from the migration plan.
steps: Capture PNG screenshots for compact collapsed, hover compact, activity compact, expanded music, and expanded app from the native preview host.
Check each screenshot for a continuous black top edge, no non-black border in compact states, and no transparent seam where the ears meet the body.
Attach the screenshot evidence to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and update the linked Phase 3 checklist rows.

description: Verify external-display scaling and preview-state switching after Phase 3 geometry is attached to the Phase 2 window shell.
steps: Run the native preview host on an internal notch display and an external non-notch display and switch through all five preview shells on each screen.
Confirm the preview shell stays attached to the active top band, scales from the Phase 2 metrics, and keeps shadow visibility limited to the documented hover and expanded states.
Update docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md and docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with the observed screen-matrix evidence.

description: Add a short Phase 3 geometry handoff note so the next queue slice can start Phase 4 window sizing cleanly.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-handoff.md with sections for Implemented Modules, Accepted Preview States, Open Risks, and Explicit Phase 4 Inputs.
Summarize which Visual files became the source of truth for tokens, metrics, and paths, and link the summary back to docs/mac-island-phase3-geometry-acceptance.md.
Check that the handoff note explicitly says business data, auth, and state-machine migration remain out of scope for this completed slice.
